creator: keramida@ceid.upatras.gr
text:
> BTW, if you approve, would you like to be a maintainer of a new
> port? Thanks!

Hi Boris,

Repo-copying the current editors/emacs to emacs22 is a good idea.  Part
of the delay in my reply was a copy of editors/emacs to editors/emacs22
before the commit.  Now that it's already in the tree, we may have to
repocopy *and* edit the RCS files a bit to strip the emacs23 commits.

But, in principle, I have no objection to the repocopy.

I'll be mostly offline, working from Singapore, until 18 Sep.  So please
go ahead with the repo-copy and any commits you want to make to bring
emacs22 in the tree.  I think it's ok to record me and/or you as the
maintainer of the emacs22 port.

In fact, since you have been doing most of the commits for patches I am
sending, it may even make sense to create an `emacs@freebsd.org' alias
and add me and you.  We should probably ask the admins about it, and see
if they feel ok about it.  Then we can add emacs@freebsd.org as the
maintainer of the ports, and you should be able to approve patches too :)
